short_description: Internal network switch is04.sr01.sao04 is down. Provisioning in Classic may be impacted
close_notes:
Network teams have replaced the faulty switch with the local data-center team on shift. Following the hardware replacement, internal teams confirmed that all stalled (test) transactions have moved on following a restart.

---

Summary details 
(All Timestamps must be in UTC) 
Issue Summary: Internal network switch is04.sr01.sao04 is down. BM & VSI provisioning in Classic may be impacted
SME Findings:  N/A
Parent INC: INC10443177
Reason of Escalation: N/A
Resolution Steps: IaaS Core (NRE) worked with DCOPS to replace the faulty switch. All stalled transactions (test) moved on after manual restart.
Signature of Issue: 
Error while preregistering with Redhat Satellite: Could not initialize Redhat api client: There was an error while communicating with the Redhat satellite. Cause: HttpCode 0 during call: 'https://rhnsatsao0401a.softlayer.local:443/katello/api/v2/organizations/customer' - curl error: Failed to connect to rhnsatsao0401a.softlayer.local port 443: Connection timed out
Jira/Github/Other Tickets: N/A
Category/Subcategory: Network Network hardware Network Switch
Timeline Summary: 
2025-11-30 21:45:00 IaaS Core (NRE) noticed an issue internal network switch is04.sr01.sao04 and created INC10443177
2025-11-30 21:54:00 IaaS Core (NRE) notified #sre-to-sre about the network outage event impacting is04.sr01.sao04
2025-11-30 22:03:27 IaaS Core (NRE) notified #core-team-discussion about the network outage event impacting is04.sr01.sao04

2025-11-30 22:15:42 IaaS Core (Compute SRE Classic)'s test Redhat VSI test provision failed at Physical License Register.
2025-11-30 22:32:55 IaaS Core (Compute SRE Classic) created Classic - Normal Sev4 INC10443479 
2025-11-30 22:34:35 IaaS Core (Compute SRE Classic) opened #warroom-classic-inc10443479
2025-11-30 22:49:01 IaaS Core (Compute SRE Classic) notified Compute Infrastructure for awareness regarding rhnsatsao0401 on #compinfra-sre-esc. The latter was already aware of the NRE incident.
2025-11-30 23:07:47 IaaS Core (Compute SRE Classic)'s test Microsoft Windows Server 2019 VSI provision (with secondary software) completed without issue
2025-11-30 23:12:58 IaaS Core (Compute SRE Classic)'s test Rocky Linux 9 VSI provision completed without issue
2025-11-30 23:15:00 IaaS Core (Compute SRE Classic) confirmed that only RHEL provisioning transactions will be impacted
2025-11-30 23:18:00 IaaS Core (NRE) is working on preparing for the switch replacement with DCOPS. No customer impact observed for provisioning
2025-11-30 23:48:00 DC started the switch replacement.
2025-12-01 00:38:44 IaaS Core (NRE) confirmed the switch replacement has completed and the ports are enabled. All failed test RHEL transactions were restarted and confirmed moving by IaaS Core (Compute SRE Classic)

long_description:
At 2025-11-30 21:45:00 UTC IBM Cloud engineers have been alerted by NRE team, to an issue with internal network switch is04.sr01.sao04. Customers attempting  to provision VSIs in SAO04 may experience longer than usual provisioning times or failed transactions. 

IBM Cloud engineers are currently investigating the issue  and will continue to provide updates until the issue is resolved.

------ Created from incident source: INC10444877 ------
Source Incident : https://watson.service-now.com/now/nav/ui/classic/params/target/incident.do%3Fsys_id%3D493730e52be1ba904a32f414d891bfcc%26sysparm_stack%3Dincident_list.do%3Fsysparm_query%3Dactive%3Dtrue


Previous incident ref: https://watson.service-now.com/now/nav/ui/classic/params/target/incident.do%3Fsys_id%3D493730e52be1ba904a32f414d891bfcc%26sysparm_stack%3Dincident_list.do%3Fsysparm_query%3Dactive%3Dtrue

Patch Snapshot resources stuck for deletion to clean up the stuck resource in LON RIAS

Region: LON
RIAS
Jumpod : Yes



1. 

for n in $(kubectl get storagesnapshots -A -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.spec.referenceVolumeID=="r018-db3b155e-8c57-49d1-8d36-257fe4664250") | select(.status.failureReason=="VolumeAlreadyLocked")| "\(.metadata.name)"'); do
name=$(echo $n | awk '{print $1}');
kubectl patch storagesnapshots -n 0f628e88c6594675bbefa097a63b9293 "$name" --type=merge -p '{"metadata":{"finalizers":[]}}';
done


2. After ~5 minutes run following command to verify all the hanging resources for deletion got cleaned up

kubectl get storagesnapshots -A -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.spec.referenceVolumeID=="r018-db3b155e-8c57-49d1-8d36-257fe4664250") | select(.status.failureReason=="VolumeAlreadyLocked")| "\(.metadata.namespace) \(.metadata.name)"'
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

long_description:
Get all nodes memory info in WDC zone1 this is an effort to investigate  the problem documented in CIO-15533: https://ibm-iaas.atlassian.net/browse/CIO-15533.
As part of this ongoing issue we need to collect data  from all the nodes in WDC zone1 for bx3d Elba nodes 

Please follow the below steps:
#Step1: Log into deployer
 
#Step2: gt -Z <mzone number or filename or path/to/mzone-yaml-file.yml>

#Step3: Clone Repo 
git clone git@github.ibm.com:Sanjana-Deepak8/node-info.git

#Step4: cd node-info/

#Step5: copy the node_list.txt attached to the Incident

#Step6: Run the script 
python3 node_info.py

#Step7: zip the logs directory
zip -r wdc1.zip logs

#Step8: Attach the zip file to the incident notes.

Remediation Steps - from the hostdpu
When the issue is with sn1
p0v1
ifconfig p0v1
sudo ip link set dev p0v1 address fe:ff:0b:00:01:e7
ifconfig p0v1
p0v0
ifconfig p0v0
sudo ip link set dev p0v0 address fe:ff:0b:ff:00:02
ifconfig p0v0

p2v1
ifconfig p2v1
sudo ip link set dev p2v1 address fe:ff:0b:00:02:e7 
ifconfig p2v1
p2v0
ifconfig p2v0
sudo ip link set dev p2v0 address fe:ff:0b:ff:00:02
ifconfig p2v0

CORE team identified that errors came from two nodes par2-qz1-sr1-rk020-s42 & par1-qz1-sr1-rk024-s02. short_description: Need this ticket to login to the prod clusters and this if for Osprey Migration.
Multiple Devices down in AMS03
close_notes: Issue is mitigated after power was restored by DC.
long_description:
On 3 December 2025 at 06:15 UTC, the frontend customer switch (FCS), fcs75a.sr02.ams03 and backend customer switch (BCS), bcs75a.sr02.ams03 spontaneously rebooted due to a scheduled power maintenance . 
The switch recovered automatically and began forwarding customer traffic normally by 3 December 2025 06:30 UTC.

During this 15 minute period, customer servers located in rack 75, server room 02, data center ams03 would have experienced a disruption in frontend network access and backend network access for the duration of this incident.

Network specialists will continue to monitor the device to determine whether additional action may be necessary to prevent a recurrence of this issue. We apologize for any issues which this incident may have caused.

EMS : https://internal.softlayer.com/EventManagement/editNotification/171201108

long_description:
Collect memory usage for NG staging node dal1-qz1-sr1-rk084-s34, this is an effort to investigate for the problem documented in CIO-15533: https://ibm-iaas.atlassian.net/browse/CIO-15533.
Node - dal1-qz1-sr1-rk084-s34
Environment - DAL staging, Zone1

Please follow the below steps:
#Step 1
Install custom tools on the node
git clone git@github.ibm.com:Timothy-Smith1/node-memory
cd node-memory/src


#Step2
Run custom tools on the node
./lsnuma
./lsnuma-avail
./lsvfs
./lsvfs-up -v
./lsvfs-down -v
./lsqemu
./hostos-memory --summary
./hostos-memory --list-vm
./hostos-memory --list-procs
./hostos-memory --list-net
./hostos-memory --list-top50
./hostos-memory --list-qemu-brief
./hostos-memory --list-tmpfs
./hostos-memory --sort-procs
./hostos-memory --sched-info
./hostos-memory --list-containerd
./hostos-memory --list-numa


#Step3
Collect kube package history on the node
cat /etc/genesis/release_bundles

#Step4
Cleanup custom tools installed above
cd ../../
rm -rf node-memory
Incident source: INC10369668 - Subhashini S

short_description: BGP Permissions group issue when IPSec VPN on Provider gateway
long_description:
When we create IPSec VPN on the provider gateway as an org user, BGP beighbor, IP prefix list and route maps are assigned with provider only permission group.

When we create IPSec VPN on the provider gateway as a system admin, BGP beighbor, IP prefix list and route maps are assigned with manage permission group

This looks like a bug. The customers are unable to update or delete IPSec VPN on the provider gateway due BGP permission issue
Incident source: https://github.ibm.com/tornado/ipops-vcd-cases/issues/1910 - Nishanth Kasturi (IaaS CORE)

long_description:
The acadia-snapshot-copy-controller is in crashloopbackoff indal12.
Jira : https://ibm-iaas.atlassian.net/browse/ACADIACP-82

Dev  team has identifie that here is a bug and as a mitigation the snapshot needs to patched 


----
There are three copies of the source snapshot. One of the copies (in us-east) was deleted, which triggered the copyActionCanceled workflow. As part of this workflow, we clean up the deleted snapshot and remove its copy reference from the source snapshot. However, for some reason that reference isn’t being removed (we’re still investigating why).
In the Snapshot CRD, all three snapshot entries still appear, but in the memory it is only shows two. This mismatch causes the pod to panic.
The immediate workaround is to manually remove the deleted snapshot’s reference from the source snapshot. 
----
Incident source: https://ibm-cloudplatform.slack.com/archives/C031RQMGWSU/p1764781916152889 - Arun Gopinathan

long_description:
Need to collect memory profiling for below pods
1. fabcon-manager-sfqbg hosted on node syd2-qz1-sr2-rk012-s10
2. fabcon-manager-7v6d4 hosted on node syd2-qz1-sr2-rk089-s15
3. fabcon-manager-vjcj2 hosted on node syd2-qz1-sr2-rk024-s42

Note: Please follow the runbook and till the data collection. Pod restart is not required. Files with name starting with "profile" would be generated while running the command. Please share the files with name starting by profile.

Note: The data collection required 2 terminal sessions.

Steps to be executed: 

- Login to deployer corresponding to mzone and set gt to the required mzone
- Create 2 terminals with sudo permissions (if required)
- Run on a terminal 1 : (Please change the pod name in the below command for different pods)
  # kubectl port-forward -n genctl fabcon-manager-sfqbg 8888:8383

Below steps are to be executed from terminal 2. 
- On another session, terminal 2:
   # curl -d '{"type":"heap", "count": 5, "seconds": 5}' -H "X-Request-ID: 123456" -H "Content-Type:application/json" 'http://localhost:8888/diagnostics/checks/profile' > profile.json

- Parse json into .prof files:
  # cat profile.json | jq -r '.check.textValues.profilingResult | fromjson | .Profiles[]' | awk '{ filename = "profile_" NR ".prof"; cmd = "base64 -d > " filename; print $0 | cmd; close(cmd) }'
Incident source: https://ibm-cloudplatform.slack.com/archives/G79AWAC0P/p1764847293317579 - Kuldeep Singh

short_description: Schematics calls should be coming from CSE IPs but are being blocked by CBR
close_notes:
(All Timestamps must be in UTC) 
Issue Summary:Schematics provisioning issues
 
At 2025-12-02 23:20 UTC   IBM Cloud engineers have been alerted by customer via case cs4422888 that Schematics provisioning and Orchestration are impacted  . IBM Cloud engineers are currently investigating the issue > and will continue to provide updates until the issue is resolved
 
SME Findings: sme-team: SME Findings
Reason of Escalation:
Resolution Steps:
Signature of Issue:
"message": "Unauthorized access. Check that you have the correct access credentials and permissions.",
"statuscode": 403

Jira/Github/Other Tickets:
Category/Subcategory:
Timeline Summary:
(All Timestamps must be in UTC)
2025-12-04
12:05 - Call started with BNPP 
12:09 - ACS Network Team has joined the call .BNPP was suspecting the CSTASK0055399 to add additional CSE IP's might have caused the issue  and asked to revert the change
12:34 - Overlay SDN SRE team has joined the call and confirmed that the IP's they have added are much later than the issue customer has reported and they are still in an inactive state. So this has been ruled out.
12:38 - CORE team has checked for the manual CR's executed last 24 hours and ruled out they weren't the issues 
13:00 - CBR team joined and analyzed the logs from their end and could not find any issues . CBR just process the inputs from Schematics where IP address is an input. CBR team suggested looking at the Schematic logs 
13:57 - Schematic Team also analyzed the logs and shared the payload for CBR evaluation and found nothing unusual.
14:08 - BNPP requested the severity to be raised to SEV1
14:18 - CORE team has opened an incident and warroom 
14:30 - Further troubleshooting going on with SDN , CBR , Schematics ,on the call 
16:02 - IKS joined the call and started troubleshooting from a CSE perspective
16:09 - IKS CSE team started troubleshooting , running tcpdumps 
16:45 - IKS CSE team identified a potential automated CR CHG14159618 could be the cause 
16:52 - IKS CSE team started reverting the CR CHG14159618 
17:00 - The changes were reverted 
17:06 - BNPP confirmed that Schematis have started working .

long_description:
Schematics -> F5 -> error is 403
There was a rule that was previously working, now its rejecting requests
IP address referenced in AT (CNR - deny) related to a specific action in schematics
Normally the IP is allowed because the IP comes from the VPC (CSE source address) - going through private endpoint.
The goal was to have all the calls coming from the CSE endpoints
All of the calls since the change (after 12am 3rd Dec) are now coming from other endpoints
Incident source: #cs4422888-bnpp-can-not-manage-schematics-workspace - Sean Caffrey (IaaS CORE)

long_description:
The cosaction delete command should be executed from the deployer, and snapshot labelling should be done from the jumpod.

sets of commands to 
1. delete the coalescevolume spec
2. patch the snapshot to remove the coalesce volume id from the status
3.label the snapshot

- 39e8ac3f9ced4b14a543b42c2d7c6dc8
    1. r018-427463ba-7d22-440e-9a21-8c64b3b26656 mzone7a7
      kubectl delete volumespec -n 0b0d7b643c374610b4b4258cb66e4d7c -l "ExternalVolumeID=r018-edf448e9-411c-41cc-bcb0-73c91a169f22"
      kubectl patch storagesnapshot -n 39e8ac3f9ced4b14a543b42c2d7c6dc8 r018-a5cf7060-f866-4594-af17-e6098ab92ca4 --type json --patch '[{"op":"replace","path":"/status/coalesceVolumeId","value":""}]'
      kubectl label snap -n 39e8ac3f9ced4b14a543b42c2d7c6dc8 r018-427463ba-7d22-440e-9a21-8c64b3b26656 "foo1=bar1"

  2. r018-7fd107ed-46ad-4cdb-9fa3-6adec2a0a245 mzone7a7
      kubectl delete volumespec -n 0b0d7b643c374610b4b4258cb66e4d7c -l "ExternalVolumeID=r018-a7f299ea-107f-4110-b25e-e887953d63be"
      kubectl patch storagesnapshot -n 39e8ac3f9ced4b14a543b42c2d7c6dc8 r018-bf4b6eb3-30be-484b-9e41-886d3f80d5ea --type json --patch '[{"op":"replace","path":"/status/coalesceVolumeId","value":""}]'
      kubectl label snap -n 39e8ac3f9ced4b14a543b42c2d7c6dc8 r018-7fd107ed-46ad-4cdb-9fa3-6adec2a0a245 "foo1=bar1"

long_description: Due to BNPP MZR DNS configuration where traffic is sent to a small number of VPCs in the MZR, and that traffic is then forwarded to Fabric Classic resolvers, during periods where there is a spike in DNS query rate, that traffic is hitting rate limits on the Classic resolvers.

short_description: Severe backup performance degradation in IBM Cloud COS
close_notes:
service team initially balanced the worklod across the other US regions to proved some immediate bandwidth improvement
2 nodes were added to the cluster (with 2 more being configured) to provide a more permanent solution
long_description:
Backups that previously completed in ~29 hours now take 56+ hours or fail entirely.
Issue spans Classic and VPC Gen2 environments, suggesting systemic COS/network problem.

short_description: RMDS-2210: Need to restart metadata agent on list of DPUs in mzone736
close_notes:

Issue Summary: RMDS-2210: Need to restart metadata agent on list of DPUs in mzone736
SME Findings: sme-team: SME Findings
Reason of Escalation:
Resolution Steps: Followed below steps to restart the metadata

1. sudo snap restart ibmcloud-vault
2. snap logs ibmcloud-vault
3. sudo snap restart ibmcloud-metadata
4. sudo diff -qs /var/snap/ibmcloud-vault/current/etc/metadata_cert.crt /run/mnt/data/system-data/var/snap/ibmcloud-metadata/common/conf/vetted-secrets//metadata_cert.crt 
5. snap logs ibmcloud-metadata

long_description:
Patch Snapshot resources stuck for deletion to clean up the stuck resource in LON RIAS

Region: LON
RIAS
Jumpod : Yes

1. 

for n in $(kubectl get storagesnapshots -A -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.spec.referenceVolumeID=="r018-b20f9db0-1c44-47c0-83d6-877392873caa") | select(.status.failureReason=="VolumeAlreadyLocked")| "\(.metadata.name)"'); do
name=$(echo $n | awk '{print $1}');
kubectl patch storagesnapshots -n 0f628e88c6594675bbefa097a63b9293 "$name" --type=merge -p '{"metadata":{"finalizers":[]}}';
done


2. After ~5 minutes run following command to verify all the hanging resources for deletion got cleaned up

Previous incident reference : https://watson.service-now.com/now/nav/ui/classic/params/target/incident.do%3Fsysparm_query%3Dnumber%3DINC10445858
kubectl get storagesnapshots -A -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.spec.referenceVolumeID=="r018-b20f9db0-1c44-47c0-83d6-877392873caa") | select(.status.failureReason=="VolumeAlreadyLocked")| "\(.metadata.namespace) \(.metadata.name)"'

short_description: For  #warroom-vpc-inc10494336 RCA
close_notes:
(All Timestamps must be in UTC)
### Issue Summary: For  #warroom-vpc-inc10494336 RCA | NFS hung in few different nodes in DAL13 - investigation for CIE INC10494336
SME Findings: 
Reason of Escalation:
Resolution Steps:High Level Update:
- Both switches ngas02.sr01.dal13 and ngas01.sr01.dal13 were reloaded and added back up in Production.
- No NFS hung issue observed since then and no more issues observed with dal3-qz1-sr1-rk363-s28.
- We are in monitoring state and will check every 30 min if more new NFS hung appears,
teams calls is closed now as agreed in call and will monitor offline if need we will reopen the call and reengage Storage and NRE team.

Signature of Issue:agonzaga@dal3-qz1-sr1-rk403-s01:~(mz737)$ cat nfs_log_andre_check | grep -i "vsff" | awk '{print $1,$2, $7}'
dal3-qz1-sr1-rk421-s04: vsff-dal1351b-sec-fz.service.softlayer.com:/cci_root/cci_vol1_ccistordal1351a/479-185ae6ac-012c-4bdd-a7e9-52729478e892 (rw,relatime,sync,vers=3,rsize=65536,wsize=65536,namlen=255,hard,nosharecache,proto=tcp,timeo=600,retrans=10,sec=sys,mountaddr=10.200.143.135,mountvers=3,mountport=635,mountproto=tcp,fsc,local_lock=all,addr=10.200.143.135)
dal3-qz1-sr1-rk421-s04: vsff-dal1351b-sec-fz.service.softlayer.com:/cci_root/virt_vol074_dal1351/0737-4189c469-1b46-4a8f-8440-a279f67d624c (rw,relatime,sync,vers=3,rsize=65536,wsize=65536,namlen=255,hard,nosharecache,proto=tcp,timeo=600,retrans=10,sec=sys,mountaddr=10.200.143.135,mountvers=3,mountport=635,mountproto=tcp,fsc,local_lock=all,addr=10.200.143.135)
Jira/Github/Other Tickets: 
Category/Subcategory:is-vpc 	pod failures 	packet loss
Timeline Summary:-Created this INC to follow up on investigation RCA for CIE INC10494336 
-NRE found stale route in two agregated Arista switches ngas02.sr01.dal13 and ngas01.sr01.dal13
-NRE costed out one by one, reload as suggested by Arista team and costed in back up into production 
-We have not seeing any issue with hung NFS since adding those back up
-We checked every hour and not finding any issue since then 


Resolution Runbook: Not Needed
Caused by Change/Incident: 
Warroom:#warroom-vpc-inc10494336
Meeting URL:
Outage Start:
Outage End:


 Other InformationOther links / facts that should be recorded   
-> This investigation lead to NRE CIe retro INC10505174 NFS server is experiencing timeouts. Upon further investigation by NRE, Net Eng and Arista provider it was established that there was inconsistent route issues seen on the ngas02 and later ngas01. A reload of the device and cost back in resolved the issue.

->This Incident broke SLO due a complicated troubleshooting engaging Several different team, Storage, Netapp, NRE, SDN, Neteng, kube, hostos. We had a CIE that also broke a SLO INC10494336 and investigation RCA also broke SLO. Issue was mitigated as mentioned above.

I suggest we add a 
-Category: Service
-Subcategory: Network Underlay
to better reflect the issue for this. I selected "packet loss" because I think it is close to what we saw with switches but is not exactly a pod failure. Please review and suggest a better category/sub. Thanks

long_description:
Need to collect memory profiling for below pods
1. fabcon-manager-sfqbg hosted on node syd2-qz1-sr2-rk012-s10
2. fabcon-manager-7v6d4 hosted on node syd2-qz1-sr2-rk089-s15
3. fabcon-manager-vjcj2 hosted on node syd2-qz1-sr2-rk024-s42

Note: Please follow the runbook and till the data collection. Pod restart is not required. Files with name starting with "profile" would be generated while running the command. Please share the files with name starting by profile.

Note: The data collection required 2 terminal sessions.

Steps to be executed: 

- Login to deployer corresponding to mzone and set gt to the required mzone
- Create 2 terminals with sudo permissions (if required)
- Run on a terminal 1 : (Please change the pod name in the below command for different pods)
  # kubectl port-forward -n genctl fabcon-manager-sfqbg 8888:8383

Below steps are to be executed from terminal 2. 
- On another session, terminal 2:
   # curl -d '{"type":"heap", "count": 5, "seconds": 5}' -H "X-Request-ID: 123456" -H "Content-Type:application/json" 'http://localhost:8888/diagnostics/checks/profile' > profile.json

- Parse json into .prof files:
  # cat profile.json | jq -r '.check.textValues.profilingResult | fromjson | .Profiles[]' | awk '{ filename = "profile_" NR ".prof"; cmd = "base64 -d > " filename; print $0 | cmd; close(cmd) }'
Incident source: https://ibm-cloudplatform.slack.com/archives/G79AWAC0P/p1764847293317 - Kuldeep Singh

short_description: Delete the server actions to start the reclaim job
close_notes:
Issue Summary: Bare metal server reclaim failed, causing disruption to compute services.
SME Findings: The server reclaim job failed, and the incident is being handled by the assigned team.
Reason of Escalation: The incident was escalated due to the customer-facing impact and the need for prompt resolution.
Resolution Steps:
Delete the server actions to start the reclaim job.
Run the command: kubectl -n genctl get serveractions -o custom-columns=NAME:.metadata.name,ACTION:.spec.action,TARGET:.spec.targetUID,STATUS:.status.actionStatus to get server actions.
Delete the server action for the specific BM server node using the command: kubectl -n genctl delete serveraction -l server_node_name=tor3-qz1-sr1-rk162-s35.
Signature of Issue: Bare metal server reclaim failure.
Jira/Github/Other Tickets: https://ibm-iaas.atlassian.net/browse/METAL-7765.
Category/Subcategory: Compute - Development/Infrastructure Services.
Timeline Summary: The incident started on 2025-12-08 at 00:00:00 UTC, and the current status is in progress.
Resolution Runbook: https://pages.github.ibm.com/cloudlab/internal-docs/bare_metal_server_reclaim_failed.html#re-running-the-reclaim.
Caused by Change/Incident: No change number found.

short_description: Observed service-error-vm_with_same_interface_and_mac_exists issue in fabcon
close_notes:
Issue Summary: Observed service-error-vm_with_same_interface_and_mac_exists issue in fabcon, indicating a problem with a virtual machine having the same interface name or MAC address.
SME Findings: The issue is related to a stale port entry that needs to be mitigated. The SME has run scripts to verify and mitigate the issue, including vm_stale_port_validation_mitigation.py.
Reason of Escalation: The issue was escalated due to the impact on the Virtual Private Cloud service, specifically the "is-vpc" component.
Resolution Steps:
Initialize Mzone.
Run python3 vm_stale_port_validation_mitigation.py to verify if there is a stale entry.
If a stale entry is found, run the script again to mitigate the issue.
Re-validate after mitigation.
Signature of Issue: The issue is characterized by the error message "VM with same interfaceName or MAC already exists" and is related to a specific port ID (UUID{7472d260-d7df-4c4e-8e1f-4d8931c3de3f[tr ` LN M 1 ?]}).
Jira/Github/Other Tickets: No specific tickets are mentioned in the provided data.
Category/Subcategory: The category is Infrastructure Services, and the subcategory is Virtual Private Cloud.
Timeline Summary: The incident was created on December 8, 2025, at 12:35:11 UTC, and the last update was at 16:20:40 UTC.
Resolution Runbook: The resolution runbook is available at https://w3.ibm.com/services/lighthouse/wikis/view/a18a9d34-1b59-405b-b480-c973100f188d/2f54860c-6784-47cb-96f0-171546db34b7.
Caused by Change/Incident: The incident was caused by a parent incident (INC10503924) and is related to a change in the fabcon configuration.
Warroom: No war room information is provided.
Meeting URL: No meeting URL is provided.

Outage End: The outage end time is not specified in the provided data.

short_description: Master node VIP not advertised on new master nodes
close_notes:
(All Timestamps must be in UTC)
Issue Summary: Master node VIP not advertised on new master nodes
SME Findings: 
Reason of Escalation: 
Resolution Steps: The issue is faced on newly expanded nodes in mzone736 as they are added with support for DualNIC which is not currently supported by Code.

Next action:
The newly expanded nodes with HostOS 7 version will be contracted and added back again with single NIC support as currently added nodes with HostOS 7 are expended with dual NIC enabled and its not supported.

Signature of Issue: New master node VIP are not advertised on the NGAS and NGCS switches.
Noticed frr.conf file syntax for BGP advertising is different compared to the old master nodes.
Jira/Github/Other Tickets: 
Category/Subcategory: is-sdn 	Custom Routes 	Incorrect/missing routes
Timeline Summary: https://ibm-cloudplatform.slack.com/archives/CQGSRSGV9/p1765226875672799?thread_ts=1765185879.441259&cid=CQGSRSGV9
Resolution Runbook: Not Needed
Caused by Change/Incident: 
Warroom:

short_description: #cm-dashboard_outage_12-8-25 create incident
close_notes:
Issue Summary: The VPC Operations Dashboard is not showing data for several regions, including CHE, LON, OSA, MON, SAO, and SYD. The issue is related to a certificate access problem, and the team is investigating the cause.
SME Findings: The SMEs have found that the issue is not related to the compute workspace, and they are investigating the possibility of a change or incident that caused the problem.
Reason of Escalation: The issue was escalated because the dashboard is not showing data, and the team needs to resolve the issue as soon as possible.
Resolution Steps: Rolling back the odz-image-version from 1.78.0 to 1.77.0 is resolving the issue. A few regions are still pending rollback. As there are no pending actions from the CORE team,closed the incident and updating it towards Jira: ODS-7610.
Signature of Issue: The signature of the issue is a certificate access problem that is preventing the VPC Operations Dashboard from showing data.
Jira/Github/Other Tickets: The following tickets are related to the issue:
ODS-7610 (investigation ticket)
CHG14156922 (change ticket)
CHG14200501 (change ticket)
Category/Subcategory: is-vpc Capacity issue Out of capacity 
Timeline Summary: The issue started on Friday, December 5th, and the team is still investigating the cause. The timeline of events is as follows:
Friday, December 5th: The issue started, and the team began investigating.
Saturday, December 6th: The team continued investigating and found that the issue was related to a certificate access problem.
Sunday, December 7th: The team continued investigating and found that the issue was not related to the compute workspace.
Monday, December 8th: The team is still investigating and taking steps to resolve the issue.
Tuesday, December 9th: Rollingback odz-image-version from 1.78.0 to 1.77.0
PR - https://github.ibm.com/nextgen-environments/global-env/pull/1891
Resolution Runbook: Not Needed
Caused by Change/Incident: The issue was caused by a change CHG14200501 
Warroom: The warroom for this issue is #warroom-vpc-inc10505654.
Meeting URL: 
Outage Start: 
Outage End:
long_description:
#cm-dashboard_outage_12-8-25 create incident

Dashboard is missing data https://opsdashboard.w3.cloud.ibm.com/graphs/d/SDgTdp6rr/vpc-capacity-review?var-interval=1h&var-interval2=1d&orgId=1&from=2025-12-08T02:30:00.000Z&to=2025-12-08T03:30:00.000Z&timezone=browser&var-DCmysql=DAL10&var-geo=$__all&var-region=$__all&var-mzone=$__all&var-zone=$__all&var-DC=$__all&var-Hw_Type=$__all&var-Hw_Gen=Gen3&var-HW_Class=bx3d&var-HW_Class=bx3d-elba&var-HW_Classmysql=$__all&var-yyyymmdd_mysql=20251208&var-yyyymmddhhmmss_mysql=20251208032502&var-fileextn_mysql=%2F20251208%2Fvpc-capacity-report-v1_Allocations-v2_20251208032502000000.parquet&var-yyyymmdd_cos=20251208&var-fileextn_cos=%2F20251208%2Fvpc-capacity-report-v1_Allocations-v2_20251208032502000000.parquet&var-fileextn_input=%2F20251208%2Fvpc-capacity-report-v1_Allocations-v2_20251208032502000000.parquet in various regions SAO, LON, TOR, SYD, OSA

short_description: Update of ACL rules is showing intermittent failures
close_notes: To migrate to the issue CSE team first created a new 3.5 database with similar configuration and generate new service credentials and dataplane certificates. Updated the credentials in Secret Manager, then brough down the CSE API server to prevent new updates to etcd. The team validate all changes thoroughly before rolling out updates to endpoints in other regions.
long_description:
Multiple patches of updating ACL rules is not working for some endpoints.This is impacting CBR policies for IKS customers which might not take effect.
Incident appears to be intermittent, impacting ~10-20% of requests

short_description: Unable to create new worker in a worker pool
close_notes:
All Timestamps must be in UTC)
Brief Summary of the issue: IKS worker (02k7_951ff839-79ee-43f0-91ca-6a62b0ea0605) is not able to communicate with master/ IKS backend.
Impacted Env(s): BNPP_EU REGION
IPOps SME Escalated: 
Reason of escalation to Service Team: 
Cause Code:
Runbooks used to resolve: 
Error logs: https://github.ibm.com/cloudlab/ipops-vpc-cases/issues/7476
Timeline summary of actions done:
2025-12-09 17:04 Upgraded to sev 3 cie
2025-12-09 17:20 checking for any dropped packets. Upon checking, the communication between worker node and vpe looks fine. 
2025-12-09 17:23:03 customer stopped the instance
2025-12-09 17:46:03 Ipops joined the customer call with IKS team
2025-12-09 18:03 update from the call IKS are reviewing there network policies that were creted ~31 h ago and 80m ago
2025-12-09 18:11 Customer has deleted these globalnetworkpolicies
2025-12-09 18:56 Customer cordoned all the nodes and they are rebooting. Will check if calico-typha pods will be running fine on these and reachable to api server.
2025-12-09 19:10 paged sdn
2025-12-09 19:33 Checking networkpolicies and globallnetworkpolicies yaml file to see if it is correctly configured.
2025-12-09 19:51 Customer edited the networkpolicy. The ports for haproxy were changed from single line to multi-line format in the yaml file.
The nodes are uncordoned

short_description: Reset the failed hostdevices on node dal2-qz1-sr2-rk075-s22
close_notes:
(All Timestamps must be in UTC)
Issue Summary: Reset the failed hostdevices on node dal2-qz1-sr2-rk075-s22
SME Findings: 
Reason of Escalation: 
Resolution Steps: Executed steps to reset the failed hostdevices
Signature of Issue: We have opened a JIRA to reset the failed ox2 profile https://ibm-iaas.atlassian.net/browse/XLR8R-910 There was a NS_INSUFFICIENT_CAPACITY issue occurring earlier due to token namespace being present on the drive which has been fixed long time back and hostdevices still remained in failed state. This inc is to reset the failed hostdevices [first we are going with dal2-qz1-sr2-rk075-s22 where currently no vsis are present].
Jira/Github/Other Tickets: 
Category/Subcategory: is-instance 	Orchestration 	Local Disk Failed
Timeline Summary: 
Resolution Runbook: Not Needed
Caused by Change/Incident: 
Warroom: 
Meeting URL: 
Outage Start: 
Outage End:
long_description:
We have opened a JIRA to reset the failed ox2 profile https://ibm-iaas.atlassian.net/browse/XLR8R-910 There was a NS_INSUFFICIENT_CAPACITY issue occurring earlier due to token namespace being present on the drive which has been fixed long time back and hostdevices still remained in failed state. This inc is to reset the failed hostdevices [first we are going with dal2-qz1-sr2-rk075-s22 where currently no vsis are present].

Step 1: Check if any vsis on the node
	    gt dal2-qz1-sr2-rk075-s22
	    virsh list
       If any, abort the process
Step 2: Check the list of nvme details on the node to check if token-ns is present using
       nvme list
Step 3: Check for orphaned device chunks
	   kubectl get deviceChunks -A --field-selector spec.Node.Name=dal2-qz1-sr2-rk075-s22
Step 4: Check for failed hostdevice details
       kubectl -n genctl get hostdevices --field-selector spec.Node.Name="dal2-qz1-sr2-rk075-s22" -o json | jq -r '.items[] | select(.status.deviceType=="ssd") | .metadata.name +" isFailed="+ ((.status.failed!=null)|tostring), .status'
Step 5: All are failing due to NS_INSUFFICIENT_CAPACITY, execute below runbook steps.

Step 6: Login to the deployer. If a bunch of hosts need remediating, consider starting a screen session first.
Step 7: cd ~/repos; rm -rf opal-ssd; git clone git@github.ibm.com:cloudlab/opal-ssd.git
Step 8: cd ~/repos/platform-inventory && git pull
Step 9: cd ~/repos/opal-ssd/helpers
Step 10: Create a file containing the list of hostnames for the Incident or what has been provided. To remediate an entire mzone, touch hostlist to create an empty file. For example: vi hostlist 
dal2-qz1-sr2-rk075-s22
Step 11: Optional: Run script in simulate mode to check activity: ./reset-failed-hostdevs.sh hostlist simulate hddc-only
Step 12: ./reset-failed-hostdevs.sh hostlist execute hddc-only
Step 13: We can check the status of newly generated hostdevices after 10 mins using
         kubectl -n genctl get hostdevices --field-selector spec.Node.Name="dal2-qz1-sr2-rk075-s22"
Incident source: https://opsdashboard.w3.cloud.ibm.com/ops/hostDevices/23314543C1BA - Prajna Lobo

short_description: VPN reporting provision failures
close_notes:
Summary(All Timestamps must be in UTC)
Issue Summary: VPN reporting provision failures
SME Findings: sme-team: CSRE is-ng-compute-sre
SME Findings
Reason of Escalation: Red hat vsi failing to provision
Resolution Steps: Provisioning failing with RedHat image. During the activation window of maintenance , provisioning of new Red Hat VSI may have failed. Connectivity for existing gateways was not affected, and existing services operated normally throughout the period.
The issue has been mitigated, and successful provisioning is expected.
Signature of Issue: Provisioning failing with RedHat image. 
Jira/Github/Other Tickets:
Category/Subcategory:
Timeline Summary:
2025-12-09 22:37:00 - Core team received notification in #ipops-production about provisioning issue in eu-de
2025-12-09 22:45:00 - CORE team checked provisioning dashboard and provisioning alerts for EU-DE
2025-12-09 22:51:00 - CORE team started teams call with VPN team 
2025-12-09 22:55:00 - CORE team were informed by VPN team that provisioning with RedHat images are failing
2025-12-09 22:57:00 - warroom-vpc-inc10515600 created for issue.
2025-12-09 23:00:00 - CORE team observed smoke test failures for VPN.
2025-12-09 23:05:00 - INC is upgraded to pCIE as there is a Red Hat maintenance update in channel
2025-12-09 23:08:00 - CORE team observed capsules server showing unhealthy in FRA 
2025-12-09 23:10:00 - CORE team asked Red Hat team implementing maintenance to join call
2025-12-09 23:10:00 - PDE7717472is-ng-compute-sre for CSRE team to join
2025-12-09 23:15:00 - RHEL jenkins job disabled as per update from team
2025-12-09 23:23:00 - Red Hat team informed upgarde is completed and asked if we are still see issues
2025-12-09 23:23:00 - Test provisioning are going correctly now after maintenance.
2025-12-09 23:30:00 - As per Red Hat team notification was sent to customers for maintenance but the failures were not expected so as Discussion with AVM and service team the CIE is set as sev4 and resolved as provisioning is going through now.

short_description: Unable to create the Secrurity group
close_notes:
Issue Summary: The issue is related to the creation of Virtual Server Instances (VSIs) failing due to a problem with listing security groups. The error message indicates an issue with unmarshalling the security group collection, specifically with the protocol field, where the value "all" is not recognized.
SME Findings: The SMEs have found that the issue is caused by a change in the API version, specifically with the introduction of a new field for "all" protocol support. They have also found that the issue can be reproduced using the vpc-go-sdk.
SME Team:  NA.
Reason of Escalation: The issue was escalated because it was causing failures in the creation of VSIs, which is a critical functionality for the customers. The SMEs were unable to resolve the issue on their own and required additional assistance.
Resolution Steps: The resolution steps include: No impact for the customer need to use correct versioning VPC SG API made an version changed yesterday on SG group and We have advised customers to use API versioning always https://cloud.ibm.com/apidocs/vpc/latest#api-versioning
Signature of Issue: The signature of the issue is the error message "Invalid value for protocol. Value all is not among the allowed values".
Jira/Github/Other Tickets: The incident is related to the following tickets:

* INC10517379: VSI Creation Failure (In Progress)
* INC10517999: VSI Creation Failure - Related to INC10517379 (Resolved)

Category/Subcategory: The incident category is "Service Disruption" and the subcategory is "VSI Creation Failure".
Timeline Summary: The timeline of the incident is as follows:

* 2025-12-10 05:42:20 UTC - The issue was first reported by the customer.
* 2025-12-10 07:27:31 UTC - The SMEs started investigating the issue.
* 2025-12-10 07:42:19 UTC - The issue was escalated to the war room.
* 2025-12-10 07:49:42 UTC - The war room discussion started.
* 2025-12-10 08:05:01 UTC - The resolution steps were agreed upon.
    Checked the logs in icl
* created the and listed the SG group in via UI and API
* Paged the SDN team PDE7718241 sdn team - @Ganeshbs joined the call
* Asked Prasad from RNOS team to join the call and joined confirmed no impact for the VPC
* 2025-12-10 08:15:01 UTC - The issue was resolved No impact for the customer need to use correct versioning VPC SG API made an version changed yesterday on SG group and We have advised customers to use API versioning always https://cloud.ibm.com/apidocs/vpc/latest#api-versioning that's the code behaviour, earlier were mapping all to icmp-tcp-udp, with new API we introduced support to new protocols, so we removed all and made it icmp-tcp-udp


Resolution Runbook: The resolution runbook is not available.
Caused by Change/Incident: The issue was caused by a change in the API version.
Warroom: The war room meeting URL is https://teams.microsoft.com/l/meetup-join/19%3ameeting_NzE4NzVhZmUtMjY0Zi00N2MyLWI1ZDAtYmUxMGExNGFkYTUx%40thread.v2/0?context=%7b%22Tid%22%3a%22fcf67057-50c9-4ad4-98f3-ffca64add9e9%22%2c%22Oid%22%3a%227ebaced2-8fba-4d9b-bea9-80fd94c826de%22%7d.
long_description:
PDNS team unable to create the VSI it is gettng failed with below error 

trans_id:b3926d1b-9c2f-4f6a-8020-66e6200e8e50
message:failed to list security groups for VSI with ID: HealthcheckVSI-eu-fr2-1:65a03f94-a3a6-4c0d-b832-72d665cd755e. RIAAS responded with 200, instead of the (expected value of) 200, however, the RIAAS response body provided no error codes
severity:error
}

short_description: Restart the acadia-action-agent-ext2 pods in SYD region
close_notes:
Issue Summary: Restart of acadia-action-agent-ext2 pods in SYD region due to stuck snapshots.
SME Findings: The incident was caused by stuck snapshots (>10 TB) from the QA account, requiring a restart of the daemonset acadia-action-agent-ext2.
Reason of Escalation: The incident was escalated due to the potential impact on customer-facing services.
Resolution Steps:
Restart the acadia-action-agent-ext2 pods in SYD region.
Cleanup of stuck snapshots (>10 TB) from the QA account.
Signature of Issue: Incident source: INC10518235 - Aaryan Raj.
Jira/Github/Other Tickets: No change number found.
Category/Subcategory: Infrastructure Services / File Block Storage.
Timeline Summary: The incident was created on 2025-12-10T11:16:29.000Z and is currently in the "in_progress" state.
Resolution Runbook: kubectl rollout restart ds acadia-action-agent-ext2 -n genctl.
Caused by Change/Incident: No change number found, but related to incident INC10518235.
Warroom: Not specified.
Meeting URL: Not specified.
Outage Start: 2025-12-10T00:00:00.000Z.
Outage End: Not specified.

we need to restart the daemonset acadia-action-agent-ext2 in order to cleanup of the stuck snapshots copies (>10 tb) from the QA account

short_description: Local Power Component Event on mon04.sr03.rk21.slh - SL018TD1 (breaker tripped)
close_notes: PWR - RPP breaker tripped with cause undetermined
long_description:
On December 10 2025 at 6h25 local time (EST GMT-5) an alert was found on mon04.sr03.rk21.slh

We processed to bring back the breaker to an optimal state by doing the following
1-Disconnected the cable on the affected outlet. 06h27
2-Reseting the breaker 06h29
3-connected each power cable 1 at a time with power reading review. 06h31
4-reaching outlet 29 we did not see any power increase and an investigation pointed out to the PS1 of switch mtor21.2.vpc.sr03.mon04 06h36

Manager was informed before and after the recovery
